conflict: the volume buyer's arrest threatens the entire chain of exposure; Marcus going dark means the ring has no intelligence about what investigators know
consequence: the operation ends — Carmen destroys federal evidence, the ring members face the return of purposelessness; Harry's private phone call suggests it's not truly over
must_land_beats: Volume buyer's arrest on local news — unrelated charge, but terrifyingly close
Marcus goes dark: phone off, no visits
Harry orders full cleanup: pills, records, phone
Carmen flushes pills in small batches, burns Agnes's notebooks behind the loading dock, breaks the prepaid phone
Agnes watching her notebooks burn — professional grief
Earl helping despite his worsening cough
Carmen: 'This is over' / Harry: 'It was good while it lasted' / Carmen: 'It wasn't good. It was necessary'
Tammy arrives early, studies Carmen, says nothing
Harry's private phone call — a number Carmen doesn't recognize

payoffs_to_land.description: Agnes's meticulous pharmaceutical notebooks, established as the operation's brain in Ch 8, are the most painful thing to destroy — watching them burn is professional grief

pressure_source: the volume buyer's arrest threatens the entire chain of exposure; Marcus going dark means the ring has no intelligence about what investigators know
secondary_character_beats: Lola's tremor is worse under stress — her body betrays the pressure her performance tries to hide
Earl leaning against walls between trips, breathing hard — his hidden illness made visible through the crisis work
state_shift: the operation ends — Carmen destroys federal evidence, the ring members face the return of purposelessness; Harry's private phone call suggests it's not truly over
